mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO and forward 60-day SPX returns is negative or not distinguishable from zero at 95% confidence over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, monetary policy is at the zero lower bound, signaling an extended accommodative stance that reduces discount rates and supports equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are negative or flat in >50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment and rise in retail sales indicates improving consumer demand and a tightening labor market, leading to stronger corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling UNRATE and rising RSAFS fails to produce positive median forward 20-day returns across all rolling 2-year windows, or the interaction term in a linear regression is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market healing, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods following a trough in UNRATE (local peak), rising PAYEMS does not produce positive forward 60-day returns more than 55% of the time over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline level, it signals weakness in manufacturing and broader economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 100, the median forward 20-day return is positive or not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of above-trend GDP growth and contained inflation (CPI stable) characterizes a 'Goldilocks' expansion, where robust demand does not overheat the economy, supporting equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising GDPC1 and stable CPIAUCSL (|monthly chg| < 0.2%) does not produce positive forward 60-day returns in >55% of quarters over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above one million units signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive near-term S&P 500 returns as the housing sector lifts the broader economy.
failure_condition: When HOUST > 1000, the median forward 5-day return is negative or the win rate is below 50% over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation increases discount rates applied to future earnings, lowering equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Rank IC of CPIAUCSL with forward 20-day returns remains above -0.02 (i.e., not consistently negative) or fails to be significantly different from zero at the 90% level.
